nj_gtr.title: General & Effective Tax Rates by County and Municipality

nj_div_taxation_general_effective_tax_ra.title: General & Effective Tax Rates by County and Municipality
nj_div_taxation_general_effective_tax_ra.notes: Effective Tax Rate is published by municipality; year-specific PDFs (e.g., 2024taxrates.pdf).

intro: Property taxes in Ridgewood, New Jersey are influenced by a combination of local municipal budgets, school district funding, and county-level tax rates. Homeowners in Ridgewood often see tax bills that differ meaningfully from nearby towns due to differences in home values, assessments, and local spending priorities.

This page provides a planning-level estimate of property taxes in Ridgewood, using the most recently available data and historical trends where available. It is designed to help homeowners and prospective buyers understand relative tax burden — not to replace official tax bills or municipal assessments.
snapshot: Based on recent data, Ridgewood has an effective property tax rate that reflects both local and county funding needs. When paired with typical home values in the area, this results in property tax bills that are generally higher than the surrounding region. Historical data shows that tax levels in Ridgewood have remained relatively stable over the past several years.

intro: Property taxes in East Orange, New Jersey are influenced by municipal budgets, school funding requirements, and Essex County tax rates. Differences in home values, assessments, and local spending priorities contribute to variations in annual tax bills across the city.

This page provides a planning-level estimate of property taxes in East Orange using the most recently available data and historical trends where available. It is intended to help homeowners and prospective buyers understand relative tax burden — not to replace official tax bills or municipal assessments.
snapshot: Based on recent data, East Orange’s effective property tax rate reflects local funding needs alongside broader county-level obligations. When paired with typical home values in the area, annual tax bills may differ meaningfully from neighboring municipalities in Essex County.

intro: Property taxes in Old Bridge Township, New Jersey are influenced by municipal budgets, school district funding, and Middlesex County tax rates. Local housing values and assessment practices also play a significant role in determining individual property tax bills.

This page provides a planning-level estimate of property taxes in Old Bridge Township using the most recently available data and historical trends where available. It is designed to help homeowners and prospective buyers understand relative tax burden — not to replace official tax bills or municipal assessments.
snapshot: Based on recent data, Old Bridge Township’s effective property tax rate reflects both local and county funding needs. When combined with typical home values in the area, property tax bills may vary compared to neighboring municipalities in Middlesex County. Historical data shows that tax levels have followed broader regional trends over the past several years.
